hypothesis: Training a universal ML interatomic potential with an auxiliary objective to predict environment-dependent atomic partial charges (e.g., Hirshfeld charges) on a MOF-representative, out-of-equilibrium dataset will reduce the MD simulation failure rate (structures with >10% volume deviation) on the MOFSimBench set by at least 30% and decrease mean absolute error in bulk modulus and heat capacity predictions by at least 20% relative to the same uMLIP trained without the charge-prediction objective.
